preview_text:
The model estimates USA at 36% to win, a modest edge over Paraguay's 33% chance. The statistical forecast sees the teams as closely matched, with the USA holding only a 3-point probability advantage—well short of favorite status in conventional terms.

The projected outcome distribution points to a competitive match. A draw at 32% represents a substantial probability, indicating that stalemate is a plausible result. The win probabilities are compressed across both teams, suggesting relatively balanced expected play with no dominant path to victory for either side.

The sportsbook consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. The market assigns USA 47%, 11 percentage points higher than the model, while pricing Paraguay 8 points lower at 26%. Oddsmakers view the fixture as considerably less even than the statistical approach does, favoring the USA more heavily and depressing draw odds to 27%. The dashboard makes no claim about which interpretation is correct; the discrepancy warrants noting for bettors and analysts.
